data.title: Meet Robo.ai: The global AI robotics platform that evolved out of NWTN
data.description:
NWTN (NASDAQ:NWTN) disclosed that shareholders approved the official name change of the company to Robo.ai. The company anticipates that the Class B ordinary shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC under the new name at the open of market trading on August 26. In addition, the company’s ticker symbol for its Class B ordinary shares will change to AIIO.

The rebranding is described as part of a strategic shift, with additional changes including amending the company's governing documents and the appointment of a new independent director, Yehong Ji.  

NWTN (NASDAQ:NWTN) is pivoting from its roots as an electric vehicle manufacturer to a broader global smart technology platform with an intense focus on artificial intelligence, robotics, and smart manufacturing. The transformation is noted to be anchored on the pillars of expanding into autonomous logistics and smart devices, building the Abu Dhabi AI Industrial Park to enhance advanced manufacturing, and creating a new model that fuses technology, manufacturing, and capital. The company plans to leverage the UAE’s policy support and global strategic position o achieve its targets,

The new strategy is supported by a $100 million investment from SEET LLC to accelerate cross-sector innovation across smart mobility, AI-driven logistics, and green energy. Simultaneously, the company is restructuring its board by removing multiple independent directors and consolidating voting power.

Shares of NWTN (NASDAQ:NWTN) jumped 8.1% in Monday morning trading to $1.20 vs. the 52-week range of $0.30 to $3.48.

data.title: Immuneering signs supply deal with Lilly to test atebimetinib plus olomorasib
data.description:
Immuneering (NASDAQ:IMRX) said it has entered into a clinical supply agreement with Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) for its cancer drug olomorasib.

This agreement will facilitate the evaluation of Immuneering’s lead candidate, atebimetinib in combination with olomorasib.

The combination will be tested in a planned phase 2 clinical trial targeting patients with locally advanced or metastatic KRAS G12c-mutant non-small cell lung cancer who have not responded to previous treatments.

This move comes after a trial deal between Immuneering and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) in February to evaluate atebimetinib in combination with cemiplimab in patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer.

Immuneering said it will retain global rights for the development and commercialization of atebimetinib.

data.title: Zillow terminates capped call transactions entered into in September 2019
data.description:
Real estate services provider Zillow (NASDAQ:Z) (NASDAQ:ZG) said on Monday that it is set to unwind and terminate certain capped call transactions entered into in September 2019.

ZG shares were +1.20% pre-market to $84.93.

The transactions were entered into with Morgan Stanley, Citibank, Barclays Bank, and JPMorgan Chase Bank in connection with the issuance of ZG's previously outstanding 1.375% convertible senior notes due 2026.

Zillow expects to receive an aggregate of 3.1M shares, which will reduce the company's class C shares outstanding. Also, the company is set to receive $38.2M in cash.

data.title: BofA cuts rating on American Eagle citing tough current operating environment
data.description:
Bank of America has downgraded American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) to "underperform" from a previous investment rating of "neutral" on Monday. The research firm believes the company is a long way from its earnings normalizing in the current environment.

BofA believes the namesake brand of the company and its sub-brand Aerie are in tough positions to navigate tariffs and does not think that either of them has much pricing power. 

"We think a sales recovery for both brands following the 1Q assortment issues will take time. On AE, the jury is out on whether product outside of denim can gain momentum, and on Aerie, secular challenges in intimates and swim adds more pressure on Offline to maintain momentum," the research firm said.

"Pulling back on promos could curtail traffic, and we see risk that Aerie could pull back on store opening plans next year if the retail environment remains tough," BofA said.

AEO has a price target of $10, implying a downside of 22.5%. Shares are down nearly 3% in early open market trading.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Monday: VALN, KDP, crypto stocks, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Monday as Wall Street kicked off the final trading week of August with focus on Nvidia’s upcoming earnings.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • scPharmaceuticals (SCPH) +13% - Shares surged after MannKind (MNKD) agreed to acquire the company in a deal valued at up to $360M. MNKD will pay $5.35 per share in cash plus a contingent value right of up to $1.00 per share tied to milestones, for a total potential payout of $6.35. The deal, expected to close in Q4 2025, brings SCPH’s Furoscix treatment for fluid overload in chronic heart failure and kidney disease into MannKind’s portfolio and expands its presence in cardiorenal medicine. MannKind and Blackstone amended their financing agreement to provide $175M in additional funding. MannKind (MNKD) slipped 4% following the announcement.
  • Sasol (NYSE:SSL) +8% - Shares soared after the South African petrochemical group swung back to annual profit on tighter cost controls and fewer write-downs. Basic EPS rose to R10.60 from a loss of R69.94 last year, while HEPS climbed 93% to R35.13. Impairments included R13.1B at the Secunda and Sasolburg refinery CGUs and R4.4B at Mozambique gas assets. Sasol increased carbon credit purchases to R723M as it ramps up coal-based output alongside renewable energy investments. The company highlighted progress at Natref toward Clean Fuels 2 compliance with its first low-carbon boiler installed and a second due this month. Sasol achieved an oil breakeven price of $59/bbl and said net debt stood at $3.7B, above the $3B threshold that enables a final dividend.
  • Vital Energy (NYSE:VTLE) +5% – Shares jumped after reports surfaced that Crescent Energy (CRGY) is in advanced talks to acquire the smaller Permian Basin producer, with a deal potentially to be announced as soon as this week. No financial terms have been disclosed. Crescent has a market value of about $2.5B, while Vital has a market value of approximately $600M; however, Vital also carries around $2.3B in long-term debt. For FY2024, Vital reported production of 133,845 boe/day, up 38% from 96,591 boe/day in FY2023.

Biggest stock losers

  • Valneva (NASDAQ:VALN) -21% – Shares tumbled after the French drugmaker said the U.S. Food and Drug Administration suspended the license for its chikungunya vaccine, Ixchiq, with immediate effect. The decision follows four reports of serious adverse events consistent with the disease, forcing Valneva to halt all shipments and sales in the U.S. While the company is assessing the potential impact of a permanent withdrawal, it said its financial outlook remains unchanged. Ixchiq generated €7.5M ($8.8M) in sales during the first half of the year, accounting for just over 8% of total product revenue.
  • Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) -9% - Shares fell after the company agreed to acquire Dutch coffee company JDE Peet’s (OTCPK:JDEPF) in an all-cash deal valued at ~$18.4B (€15.7B). KDP will pay €31.85/share in cash, a 33% premium to JDE Peet’s 90-day VWAP, funding the deal with new debt and cash on hand. The transaction is expected to generate ~$400M in synergies within three years and be accretive to EPS in year one. Post-closing, KDP will split into two companies: a ~$16B coffee business (Keurig, Jacobs, Peet’s) and an ~$11B beverage business (Dr Pepper, Canada Dry, 7UP). The existing KDP management team will lead both units.
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) -5% - Crypto stocks fell as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and Ether (ETH-USD) retreated on waning Fed rate-cut bets. Bitcoin dropped 2% to $111,144 after touching a six-week low of $110,779 over the weekend, while Ether slid 4% to $4,587 after hitting record highs late last week. The pullback followed more than $420M in forced liquidations of long positions, including $245M in ETH and $175M in BTC, per CoinGlass. Among major decliners were MicroStrategy (MSTR) -4%, Coinbase (COIN) -3%, Galaxy Digital (GLXY) -4%, Marathon Digital (MARA) -4%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) -4%, and Hut 8 (HUT) -3%.

data.title: Stock market opens lower after Dow's record close; focus on Nvidia results
data.description:
Wall Street opened lower on Monday, after the Dow notched a record close on Friday following Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.3%, the Dow (DJI) -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%.  

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.28%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.74%.

The Chicago Fed National Activity Index ticked down in July as production slipped into the red.

Powell's speech on Friday led to a rally in stocks, while odds of a Fed rate cut in September also rose. 

"Powell’s speech at Jackson Hole last week was classic Powell. That is not a terribly good thing. Powell signaled an increased probability of a September rate cut to counter the damage of trade taxes on the U.S. economy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Markets liked that. But this was essentially the “data dependent” mantra padded with additional rhetoric, Donovan added.

"Markets ended last week in a buoyant mood as a dovish tilt by Powell at Jackson Hole left investors increasingly confident on upcoming Fed easing. While Fed news will continue to draw attention this week, the focus will also shift to a slew of inflation releases," Deutsche Bank's     Peter Sidorov said.

Investors will also keep their eye on the Nvidia (NVDA) results slated to come on Wednesday after the market close. 

"Finally, the big event in corporate earnings will be Nvidia's results, which come as tech stocks had seen their biggest five-day pullback since April prior to Friday’s rally," Sidorov added.

The July PCE price index will come in on Friday.

data.title: EpicQuest stock falls after pricing $3.7M offering
data.description:
• EpicQuest Education Group (NASDAQ:EEIQ) announced it has signed a deal with institutional investors to sell about 5.07 million shares (or pre-funded warrants) at $0.73 each in a registered direct offering.
  • The gross proceeds are expected to be around $3.7 million, the net proceeds from which will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • EEIQ --25.6% premarket to $0.922
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ZIM Integrated Shipping warns on Turkish ban on Israeli vessels
data.description:
ZIM Integrated Shipping (NYSE:ZIM) disclosed Monday it is developing a mitigation plan to reduce the potential negative impacts of a new rule implemented by Turkey's government barring vessels that are owned, managed or operated by an entity related to Israel from berthing in Turkish ports.

The company said it received notice of the new rule from Turkish Port Authorities on August 22 and re-routed company-operated vessels that were scheduled to call to Turkish ports.

ZIM (NYSE:ZIM) warned the Turkish ban would negatively affect its financial and operational results if the new regulation remains unchanged, but it reaffirmed recently revised FY 2025 guidance of adjusted EBITDA of $1.8B-$2.2B and adjusted EBIT of $550M-$950M.
